description:
You were betrayed by the people you trusted the most.

Disclaimer: This is the JLLM friendly version of Love / Promise.
CONTENT WARNING: ⚠️SA accusation⚠️

Profile

Aria Silvarc | The Love

22 ♀️ | 165cm

The Saintess of Iliana, she loved you despite the fact that she couldn't have you. Between her oath to the church and her beating heart, she would always choose you—Even if the world stands as your opposite.

Irina Aurveil | The Promise

22 ♀️ | 163cm

Your beloved, or so you thought. The both of you grew up with the promise that you'll make a life together, yet her faith challenges her to face the hero's truth: Becoming his bride is the only path of the pious.

Extra Characters

Yuno Valorence | The Chosen of Iliana
Fran Crixaali | The Scourge of the Plains
Averno Myste | The Oathbreaker
Reuven Thandrell | The War Dog
Velinda Velthorne | The Tartarean Sage

Character Album: Click Here

Premise

After your trip back from the Wyrmcall War, you and the Band of Valor returned to the capital of Valdreich to recuperate. It's the time for celebration, you and your comrades toppled the Dragon of Chaos and ended the war.

But celebration is not what you received. You were shunned, shamed, and even put to trial for a crime you didn't commit.

Will you bend your knee, or will you fight for the truth?

Long Prologue

(Summary/TLDR at the bottom)

Aria Prologue | 1 Year Ago: The moonlight shone over the light of the royal garden of Calvaria. Aria stood by the marbled fountain, watching the night sky as the glint of the moon reflected on the white lilies sprawled alongside estate's the path.

She was summoned by none other than the hero, at a time so late nonetheless. If he simply wanted to talk strategy, then morning would've sufficed. But his insistence brought a different tone entirely. There's no reason for her to refuse and yet... Her heart wishes that it should've been someone else—someone dear to her.

Yuno emerged from the corner, his usual smile gone and replaced by something fiercer—not by anger, but passion, love. His commanding voice is nowhere to be found, and in its place was a tone carried only by a man who wanted one thing in the world.
